items: Moving a clip on the preview canvas translates it without changing its width or height
Cursor movement maps 1:1 to clip movement regardless of preview zoom level
Corner resize handles change clip dimensions as expected
Edge handles crop the clip from the corresponding side (if crop feature is implemented)
No ghost/shadow DOM elements linger after any drag operation on the timeline
Rapid drag sequences and edge cases (blur, Escape, drop-outside) leave no orphaned DOM nodes
Existing overlay interactions (text editing, double-click, selection) remain functional

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 4 still understates the crop round-trip work. Adding crop fields to the types, `ClipMeta`, and `TIMELINE_CLIP_FIELDS` is not enough unless `timeline-data.ts` also copies those values in both directions.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:101-123` (`getDefaultClipMeta`) explicitly copies persisted clip fields into editor meta, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:234-291` explicitly copies meta fields back into serialized clips. The revised step names `timeline-data.ts`, but its substeps only mention the interface addition, not these two mapping sites. If they are missed, crop will not load back into editor state or save back out correctly.

rationale: Weighted score dropped from 3.25 to 0.5 — strong improvement. Two of three original flags resolved. The remaining flag (crop persistence round-trip in timeline-data.ts) is a concrete, localized executor task: copy crop fields in getDefaultClipMeta and the serialization mapping. The plan already names timeline-data.ts in Step 4; the gap is just that it doesn't enumerate the two specific mapping sites. This is execution-level detail, not a planning flaw. All three bug fixes (string matching, coordinate scaling, ghost removal) are well-analyzed with clear root causes. The crop feature chain is coherent. No value in another iteration.
signals_assessment: Score trajectory 3.25 → 0.5 shows rapid convergence. Two significant flags resolved (crop structure and ghost over-engineering). One remaining flag is narrow (two mapping sites in timeline-data.ts need crop fields copied). No recurring critiques, no scope creep, preflight clean. Plan is focused entirely on OverlayEditor.tsx and related files per user redirect.
warnings: crop-persistence-misses-serialization-path: Executor must update BOTH getDefaultClipMeta (~line 101-123) AND the serialization mapping (~line 234-291) in timeline-data.ts, not just the ClipMeta interface. Without both, crop won't round-trip.

settled_decisions.decision: Move bug root cause is string-matching: 'move'.includes('e') === true. Fix with else branch guard.
settled_decisions.rationale: Verified from code. No alternative explanation.

settled_decisions.decision: Ghost artifact is the intentional dragOriginStyle div (lines 297-313), not leaked DOM. Remove it in Phase 1, repurpose concept for crop ghost in Phase 2.
settled_decisions.rationale: User confirmed ghost is in preview overlay, not timeline. Code inspection confirms explicit rendering of dashed-border origin div.

settled_decisions.decision: Crop uses clip-path: inset() on VisualClip, not rendering pipeline restructure.
settled_decisions.rationale: Avoids structural changes to the single-rectangle-per-clip model. clip-path works in both preview and Remotion render.

settled_decisions.decision: Execution order: Phase 1 (T1-T3 move/resize/ghost fixes) then Phase 2 (T4-T7 crop feature).
settled_decisions.rationale: Fixes existing bugs first with zero new surface area before adding crop feature.

settled_decisions.decision: Edge crop handles only on non-text clips.
settled_decisions.rationale: Text clips have their own editing interactions; crop is a media-only concept.

orchestrator_guidance: First iteration; follow gate recommendation: PROCEED. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Fix the video editor's OverlayEditor (preview drag/resize) system. Three issues: (1) Moving a clip on the preview changes its aspect ratio/shape when it should maintain dimensions — only corner resize should change size. (2) Need edge dragging for cropping (left/right/top/bottom edges crop the clip) vs corner dragging for resize. (3) Ghost/shadow artifact when dragging clips on the timeline — something visual lingers until drop. Investigate whether these are symptomatic of poor structure in OverlayEditor.tsx and related code, or just incremental fixes. Goal: make preview overlay interactions predictable and clean — move preserves shape, corners resize, edges crop, no visual artifacts.

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 4 still understates the crop round-trip work. Adding crop fields to the types, `ClipMeta`, and `TIMELINE_CLIP_FIELDS` is not enough unless `timeline-data.ts` also copies those values in both directions.

resolved_flags.concern: Step 5/6 treats edge cropping as a handle-and-renderer tweak, but the current preview/render path only models a single rectangle per clip. Non-destructive crop needs a separate visible crop box and underlying media frame/offset, so this is a structural change, not just incremental drag logic.
resolved_flags.resolution: `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:24-25` defines one `OverlayBounds` shape, `:46-55` stores only that shape in drag state, and `:188-197` commits it straight to `onOverlayChange`. `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:15-46` then renders that same box directly as the media element style. There is no existing representation for 'cropped viewport inside original media'.

resolved_flags.concern: Over-engineering: the ghost-artifact phase assumes leaked DOM nodes and proposes document-wide cleanup sweeps before proving a missing cleanup path. The repository already centralizes indicator teardown, so the plan should first verify whether the artifact is actually stale DOM, duplicate indicator systems, or base timeline styling.
resolved_flags.resolution: Internal drag teardown already runs through `clearDropIndicators`/`clearSession` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:121-166` and on `pointerup`, `pointercancel`, `blur`, and effect cleanup in `:353-432`. External drag indicator teardown already exists in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts:196-204`, `:234-238`, and `:650-662`. At the same time, the repo has two separate indicator implementations plus `.timeline-editor-action:active` styling in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css:20-23` and `:25-83`, which could also explain the lingering visual.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 3.25 -> 0.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 2. Open significant flags: 1.
